title: केतु कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री केतु कवचम् ॥

अस्य श्री केतु कवचस्य त्र्यम्बक ऋषिः, अनुष्टुप् छन्दः, श्री केतुर्देवता, केतु प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः।

चित्रवर्णं किरीटिनं मयूरध्वजसंयुतम्।
ध्वजाकारं महाकायं केतुं वन्दे शिखामणिम्॥ १॥

केतुः शिरसि मे रक्षेल्ललाटं चित्रवर्णकः।
नेत्रे धूम्रवपुः पातु श्रवणौ सर्ववेदनः॥ २॥

घ्राणं तु पातु मे केतुर्मुखं दीप्तिमुखः सदा।
जिह्वां मे सिंहिकासूनुर्दन्तान् मे मणिभूषणः॥ ३॥

कण्ठं पातु मयूरेशः स्कन्धौ मे रौद्रदेहवान्।
भुजौ मे रक्तनेत्रश्च करौ मे ज्वालयान्वितः॥ ४॥

वक्षो मे रक्षतु सदा रुद्रमूर्तिर्महाग्रहः।
हृदयं चात्मपुत्रश्च उदरं मे विशालकः॥ ५॥

नाभिं मे रक्षतु सदा कर्मसाक्षी महाग्रहः।
कटिं मे धूम्रवर्णश्च ऊरू मे शान्तिकारकः॥ ६॥

जानुनी मे ज्ञानरूपो जङ्घे मे ज्वलनप्रभः।
पादौ मे पद्मसंकाशौ सर्वाङ्गं पातु केतुकः॥ ७॥

प्राच्यां रक्षतु मे केतुराग्नेय्यां धूम्रवर्णकः।
याम्ये चित्रस्वरूपश्च नैऋत्यां भयनाशनः॥ ८॥

वारुण्यां रक्तनयनो वायव्यां च महाबलः।
कौबेर्यां ग्रहराजश्च ऐशान्यां मोक्षदायकः॥ ९॥

ऊर्ध्वं मे रक्षतु सदा केतुर्ज्ञानप्रदायकः।
अधो मे रक्षतु सदा शिखिवाहनसंस्थितः॥ १०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
केतोरिदं कवचमार्तिहरं महत्
यः पाठयेद् भुवि सदैव शुभं लभेत्।
केतुग्रहेण जनिताः समुपद्रवा
ये तैर्न पीड्यत इहात्र न संशयः॥

मोक्षमार्गप्रदं चैव केतोः कवचमुत्तमम्।
व्याधिनाशनमैश्वर्यं केतुकवचधारणात्॥

॥ इति श्री केतु कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ketu Kavacham ||

Asya Shri Ketu Kavachasya Tryambaka Rishiph, Anushtup Chhandah, Shri Ketur-Devata, Ketu Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Chitravarnam kiritinam Mayura-dhvaja-samyutam |
Dhvajakaram mahakayam Ketum vande Shikhaminim || 1 ||

Ketuh shirasi me rakshet-lalatam Chitravarankakah |
Netre Dhumravapuh patu shravanau Sarva-vedanah || 2 ||

Ghranam tu patu me Ketur-mukham Diptimukhah sada |
Jihvam me Simhika-sunur-dantan me Mani-bhushanah || 3 ||

Kantham patu Mayureshah skandhau me Raudra-dehavan |
Bhujau me Raktanetrash-cha karau me Jvalayaanvitah || 4 ||

Vaksho me rakshatu sada Rudra-murtir-mahagrahah |
Hridayam cha-Atma-putrash-cha udaram me Vishalakah || 5 ||

Nabhim me rakshatu sada Karma-sakshi Mahagrahah |
Katim me Dhumravarnash-cha uru me Shanti-karakah || 6 ||

Januni me Jnana-rupo janghe me Jvalana-prabhah |
Padau me Padma-sankasho sarvangam patu Ketukah || 7 ||

Prachyam rakshatu me Ketur-Agneyam Dhumra-varnakah |
Yamye Chitra-svarupash-cha Nairityam Bhaya-nashanah || 8 ||

Varunyam Rakta-nayano Vayavyam cha Mahabalah |
Kauberyam Graha-rajash-cha Aishanyam Moksha-dayakah || 9 ||

Urdhvam me rakshatu sada Ketur-Jnana-pradayakah |
Adho me rakshatu sada Shikhi-vahana-samsthitah || 10 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Ketor-idam kavacham-arti-haram mahat
yah pathayed bhuvi sadaiva shubham labhet |
Ketu-grahena janitah samupadrava
ye tair-na pidyata ihatra na samshayah ||

Moksha-marga-pradam chaiva Ketoh kavacham-uttamam |
Vyadhi-nashanam-aishvaryam Ketu-kavacha-dharanat ||

|| Iti Shri Ketu Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
इस श्री केतु कवच के त्र्यम्बक ऋषि हैं, अनुष्टुप् छन्द है, श्री केतु देवता हैं, केतु की प्रसन्नता हेतु जप में विनियोग है।

चित्रवर्ण, किरीटधारी, मयूरध्वज से युक्त, ध्वजाकार, विशालकाय केतु, शिखामणि को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ॥ १॥

केतु मेरे शिर की रक्षा करें, ललाट की चित्रवर्णक करें, नेत्रों की धूम्रवपु रक्षा करें, कानों की सर्ववेदन रक्षा करें॥ २॥

नाक की केतु रक्षा करें, मुख की दीप्तिमुख सदा रक्षा करें। जिह्वा की सिंहिकासुनु, दन्तों की मणिभूषण रक्षा करें॥ ३॥

वक्ष की रुद्रमूर्ति महाग्रह सदा रक्षा करें। नाभि की कर्मसाक्षी महाग्रह सदा रक्षा करें। कटि की धूम्रवर्ण, ऊरुओं की शान्तिकारक रक्षा करें॥ ५-६॥

पूर्व में केतु, अग्निकोण में धूम्रवर्णक, दक्षिण में चित्रस्वरूप, नैऋत्य में भयनाशन, वायव्य में महाबल, ईशान में मोक्षदायक रक्षा करें॥ ८-९॥

केतु का यह कवच पीड़ाहारक है। जो पृथ्वी पर इसका पाठ करता है, सदा शुभ प्राप्त होता है। मोक्षमार्ग प्रदान करने वाला, रोगनाशक, ऐश्वर्य देने वाला केतु कवच उत्तम है॥

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीketukavachamमन्त्रस्य त्र्यम्बक ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री केतु देवता। ॐ कें केतवे बीजम्। केतु शक्तिः। श्री केतु प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides divine armor protection from all Ketu afflictions
Shields body and all ten directions from Ketu's negative influence
Invokes the path of moksha (liberation) through ketu's grace
Traditionally recited for removal of chronic and mysterious diseases caused by ketu
Traditionally recited for removal of ketu dosha in horoscope
Enhances spiritual knowledge and occult powers
Invokes the blessing of prosperity and destroys all fears
description: Dedicated to the Nine Planets (Navagraha), the Ketu Kavacham (केतु कवचम्) is a divine armor prayer for protection revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Brahmanda Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to grant the path of moksha (liberation) through Ketu's grace, remove chronic and mysterious diseases caused by Ketu, and destroy Ketu dosha in horoscope. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) to invoke divine grace. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ketu Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ketu Kavacham in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Protective armor prayer for removing Ketu dosha and spiritual growth.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ketu Kavacham and which planet does it invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ketu Kavacham is a Sanskrit kavacha (armor) stotra of ten verses dedicated to Ketu, the southern lunar node and ninth of the Navagraha in Vedic astrology. The text is preserved in the Brahmanda Purana tradition and is attributed in its viniyoga to Tryambaka Rishi, in Anushtup chhanda, with Ketu as the presiding devata. Ketu is iconographically described in the kavacham as chitravarna (multi-coloured), kiritin (crowned), mayura-dhvaja-samyutam (carrying the peacock banner), and dhvajakara mahakaya (huge flag-like body). The kavacha then assigns specific Ketu-name forms to protect each part of the body from head to feet, plus all ten directions. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; Graha-stotra-sangraha.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Ketu in Vedic astrology and how is he different from Rahu?
seoFaqs.answer: Ketu is the southern lunar node — the descending point where the Moon's orbit crosses the ecliptic from north to south — while Rahu is the corresponding northern (ascending) node. In the puranic narrative both are halves of the asura Svarbhanu who was beheaded by Vishnu's Sudarshana chakra during the churning of the ocean: the head became Rahu and the tailed body became Ketu. Astrologically Ketu is a chhaya-graha (shadow planet) representing moksha, detachment, past-life karma, sudden mysterious illnesses, occult knowledge, spiritual insight, and the dissolution of ego. Where Rahu craves outward worldly experience, Ketu pulls inward toward renunciation and the unseen. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Phaladeepika.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Ketu Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ketu Kavacham is most powerful when recited on Tuesdays (Mangalvar) and Saturdays (Shanivar), the days governed by Mars and Saturn which share lordship-affinity with Ketu in classical astrology. It is also strongly recommended during Ketu mahadasha and antardasha, during Ketu transits across the natal Moon, Sun or ascendant, on every Amavasya (new moon, sacred to nodal pacification), and during eclipses (when Rahu and Ketu are most active). The optimal recitation time is during the Brahma muhurta or after the morning bath, ideally facing south or south-east. (Sources: Graha-shanti tradition; Lal Kitab and Parashari remedial astrology.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ketu Kavacham bestow according to its phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti of the Ketu Kavacham declares that one who recites this kavacha daily on earth always obtains auspiciousness (sadaiva shubham labhet) and is never afflicted by the troubles arising from Ketu (ketu-grahena janitah samupadrava ye tair na pidyata — without doubt). The text further states that this kavacha is moksha-marga-pradam (bestower of the path to liberation), vyadhi-nashanam (destroyer of disease), and aishvarya-pradam (giver of prosperity). Traditional commentaries extend the benefits to relief from chronic and undiagnosable illnesses (a classical Ketu signature), removal of past-life karmic obstacles, protection from accidents, growth of intuition and occult insight, and ultimately spiritual liberation. (Sources: Ketu Kavacham phalashruti; Brahmanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ketu beej mantra and how many times should it be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ketu beej mantra is Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah, while the simpler one used in the Ketu Kavacham viniyoga is Om Kem Ketave Namah. The Vedic Ketu mantra is Om Kayanaschitra Abhuvaduti Sadavridhah Sakha Kayashachistaya Vrita. Traditional remedial astrology prescribes 17,000 to 18,000 repetitions of the beej mantra over the course of forty days (one mandala) to constitute a complete Ketu shanti japa. The recitation should be undertaken during Ketu hora, ideally with rudraksha mala, after taking a sankalpa with the specific intention of pacifying Ketu's malefic effects in the natal chart. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; standard graha-japa manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What are common signs of an afflicted Ketu in the birth chart?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical astrology and traditional remedial texts identify several signatures of an afflicted Ketu, which the Ketu Kavacham is recited to mitigate. These include sudden and undiagnosable health problems, skin diseases and digestive disorders, headaches and neurological symptoms, fluctuating spiritual states, sudden detachment from family or career, mysterious losses, recurrent dreams of snakes or beheaded figures, problems through children especially in the fifth house, and Kala Sarpa Dosha when all planets are hemmed between Rahu and Ketu. Conjunction of Ketu with the Moon, Mars or Sun in the lagna, second, fourth, seventh or twelfth house typically requires sustained pacification. Consultation with a qualified Vedic astrologer is recommended before undertaking remedies. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Phaladeepika; Lal Kitab.)
